questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: If the circles <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>16</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo><msqrt><mn>6</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>k</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><msqrt><mn>6</mn></msqrt></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>, touch internally at the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>β</mi><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>6</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to _______.
questions.solution:
The circle x2+y2+6x+8y+16=0 has centre -3,-4 and radius 9+16-16=3 units.

The circle x2+y2+23-3x+24-6y= k+63+86,k>0 has centre 3-3,6-4 and radius 3-32+6-42+k+63+86=k+34

Given that these two circles touch internally, so

distance between their centres=difference of radii

3+6=k+34-3

⇒k+34-3=±3

Here, k=2 is only possible value  ∵  k>0

Now the equation of common tangent to both the circles is given by 23x+26y+16+k+63+86=0

∵  k=2 then equation becomes 

x+2y+33+3+42=0     ⋯i

∵  α,β are foot of perpendicular from -3,-4 to this common tangent, then

α+31=β+42=--3-42+3+42+331+2

∴ α+3=-3 & β+42=-3

⇒α+32=9 and β+62=16

Hence, α+32+β+62=25
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A rectangle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> with end points of the one of its sides as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is inscribed in a circle. If the equation of a diameter of the circle is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then the area of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is _____.
questions.solution:
Equation of line AB will be 

y-2=2x-1⇒2x-y=0

Now the given diameter is parallel to AB

Then, the distance between diameter and line AB

=422+12=45

Thus, BC=2×45=85

Also AB=1-32+2-62=20=25

Area of the rectangle=AB×BC=85×25=16
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Let the mirror image of a circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>c</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>:</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>  in line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></math> be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>c</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>:</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><mi>g</mi><mi>x</mi></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><mi>f</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>38</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> is the radius of circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>c</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><msup><mi>r</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to ______
questions.solution:
Let the mirror image of a circle c1:x2+y2-2x-6y+α=0  in line y=x+1 be c2:5x2+5y2+10gx +10fy+38=0. If r is the radius of circle c2, then α+6r2 is equal to ______

Now Image of centre c1≡1,3 in x-y+1=0 is given by

x1-11=y1-3-1=-21-3+112+12

⇒x1=2,y1=2

So, centre of circle c2≡2,2

Now radius of circle c2 will be  4+4-385=25=r

Now radius of c12=radius of c22 as they are image to one another,

⇒12+32-α=25

⇒10-α=25⇒α=485

Now putting the value of α & r in α+6r2 we get,

α+6r2=485+125=12
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Consider a circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><msup><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mo>–</mo><mo> </mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo> </mo><mo>–</mo><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn><mi>y</mi><mo> </mo><mo>=</mo><mo> </mo><mi>α</mi><mo> </mo><mo>–</mo><mo> </mo><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo></math> Let its mirror image in the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></math> be another circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>:</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mo>–</mo><mn>10</mn><mi>f</mi><mi>x</mi><mo> </mo><mo>–</mo><mo> </mo><mn>10</mn><mi>g</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mo> </mo><mn>36</mn><mo> </mo><mo>=</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo></math>  Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> be the radius of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> . Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mo> </mo><mi>r</mi></math> is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi></math>
questions.solution:
Given,

C1 : x2+ y2–4x– 2y+5 – α= 0

So, its centre will be, O1=2, 1 and radius =α

And C2 : 5x2+5y2–10fx–10gy+36=0

⇒C2 : x2+y2–2fx–2gy +365=0

So, Centre O2 = f, g and radius  r=f2+g2-365

Also given O2 is reflection of O1 in 2x–y+1=0, so image formula we get,

⇒f-22=g-1-1=-2·2×2-1+122+12

⇒f=-65 and g=135

So, radius r=-652+1352-365=255=1

⇒r=1 and α =1 as they both are same radius circle,

Hence, r+α=2.
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Let the abscissae of the two points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math> be the roots of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>r</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>p</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and the ordinates of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math> be the roots of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>s</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>q</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. If the equation of the circle described on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mi>Q</mi></math> as diameter is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>11</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>14</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>22</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>r</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>s</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>q</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>p</mi></math> is equal to ______.
questions.solution:
Let the roots of 2x2-rx+p=0 are x1, x2 and roots of y2-sy-q=0 are y1, y2

So, x1+x2=r2, x1x2=p2, y1+y2=s, y1y2=-q

Equation of the circle with PQ as diameter will be 

x-x1x-x2+y-y1y-y2=0 

i.e. 2x2+y2-rx-2sy+p-2q=0 

On comparing with the given equation r=11,s=7

p-2q=-22

∴ 2r+s-2q+p=22+7-22=7
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: An ellipse <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi><mo>:</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> passes through the vertices of the hyperbola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>H</mi><mo>:</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>49</mn></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>64</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math>. Let the major and minor axes of the ellipse <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math> coincide with the transverse and conjugate axes of the hyperbola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>H</mi></math>. Let the product of the eccentricities of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>H</mi></math> be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>l</mi></math> is the length of the latus rectum of the ellipse <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>113</mn><mi>l</mi></math> is equal to _______.
questions.solution:
Given,

Hyperbola:y264-x249=1

And ellipse E:x2a2+y2b2=1 passes through the vertices of the hyperbola H:x249-y264=-1, so vertices will be V≡0,±8

So b2=64

Now eccentricity of hyperbola will be eH=1+a2b2=1+4964

And eccentricity of ellipse x2a2+y2b2=1 will be

eE=1-a2b2=1-a264

And using b=8

We get, eH×eE=12 (given)

⇒1-a264×1138=12

⇒64-a2×113=32

⇒64-a2=322113

⇒a2=64-322113

Now length of latus rectum will be l=2a2b=2864-322113=1552113

⇒113l=1552
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Let <math><mi>A</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mi>B</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math><mi>C</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> be the vertices of a triangle <math><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi><mi>C</mi><mo>.</mo></math> If <math><mi>P</mi></math> is a point inside the triangle <math><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi><mi>C</mi></math> such that the triangles <math><mi>A</mi><mi>P</mi><mi>C</mi><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mi>A</mi><mi>P</mi><mi>B</mi><mi></mi></math> and <math><mi>B</mi><mi>P</mi><mi>C</mi></math> have equal areas, then the length of the line segment <math><mi>P</mi><mi>Q</mi><mo>,</mo><mi></mi></math> where <math><mi>Q</mi></math> is the point <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>7</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi></mi></math> is
questions.solution:
P must be centroid of ∆ABC
∴P176,83⇒PQ=2462+93=5 units
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> be a parabola with vertex <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and focus <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> be its mirror image with respect to the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn></math>. Then the directrix of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo></math> _____.
questions.solution:
Plotting the diagram as per given data we get,

P1: Directrix

x+2y=k

x+2y-k=0

3+4-K5=5

7-k=5

7-K=57-K=-5k=2k=12AcceptedRejected Passes through focus

D1=x+2y=2=x+2y=6D2=x+2y=C⇒d⇒d⇒c=10
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mn>3</mn><msqrt><mi>a</mi></msqrt></mfrac><mo>,</mo><msqrt><mi>a</mi></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>, be a fixed point in the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mi>y</mi></math>-plane. The image of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi></math>-axis be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> and the image of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>D</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>cos</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>,  is a point in the fourth quadrant such that the maximum area of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∆</mo><mi>A</mi><mi>C</mi><mi>D</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn></math> square units, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> is equal to _____
questions.solution:
A=3a,a

B=-3a,a is the image of A on y-axis

C=-3a,-a is the image of B in x-axis

D3cosθ,asinθ

Area of triangle ACD will be

123aa1-3a-a13cosθasinθ1

=3acosθ-sinθ

The maximum area of triangle is 3a2

i.e. 3a2=12

⇒a=8
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A ray of light passing through the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> reflects on the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>X</mi></math>-axis at point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and the reflected ray passes through the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> be the point that divides the line segment <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>Q</mi></math> internally into the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>1</mn></math>. Let the co-ordinates of the foot of the perpendicular <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math> from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> on the bisector of the angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mi>A</mi><mi>Q</mi></math> be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then, the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>β</mi></math> is equal to _____.
questions.solution:
By observation we know the point Aα,0 and β=y-coordinate of R=2×4+1×02+1=83     ⋯1

Now P' is image of P in y=0 which will be P'2,-3

∴  Equation of P'Q is y+3=4+35-2x-2 

i.e. 3y+9=7x-14

Now P'Q meets x-axis at A

so A≡237,0 by solving with y=0

∴α=237     ⋯2

By 1,2, we get

7α+3β=23+8=31
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A triangle is formed by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>X</mi></math>-axis, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Y</mi></math>-axis and the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>60</mn></math>. Then the number of points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> which lie strictly inside the triangle, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> is an integer and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>b</mi></math> is a multiple of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math>, is _____ .
questions.solution:
Given:

3x+4y=60

⇒x20+y15=1

If x=1,y=574=14.25

So, points are

1,11,2-1,14  ⇒14 points.

If x=2,y=272=13.5

So, points are

2,22,4…2,12  ⇒6 points.

If x=3,y=514=12.75

So, points are

3,33,6-3,12  ⇒4 points.
